ES HOW TO HELP
Venezuela · July 28, 2024
Failed
0 Official CNE actas
vs
25,575 Citizen actas with verifiable QR codes

The CNE published zero actas. Citizens published 25,575. That difference has a name.

28J 2024 Electoral fraud OAS · Carter Center · OHCHR
→
10 Jan 2025 Constitutional rupture Jan. 10, 2025 inauguration without verifiable actas. The actas remain unpublished.

646 days of impunity since the Art. 155 LOPRE deadline expired

Read the case ↓ Go to the actas
Official/legal documents linked: 7 · Last updated: 2026-06-06 · Methodology & sources
ACT I — WHAT THE LAW REQUIRES

The Legal Obligation

No need to take anyone's word for it. This is what the CNE itself was required to do and did not.

48h Totalization

VIOLATED
Expired since Jul 30, 2024 — Art. 146 LOPRE (48 hours from July 28)

Time in violation

30d Electoral Gazette

VIOLATED
Expired since Aug 27, 2024 — Art. 155 LOPRE (30 days from July 28)

Time in violation

Post-Election Audits

0 OF 3 PERFORMED

Required by the CNE's own official schedule.

  • Jul 29–31 Telecommunications Phase II NOT performed
  • Aug 2 Citizen Verification Phase II NOT performed
  • Aug 5–8 Electoral Data Phase II NOT performed
View in detail →
LOPRE (Georgetown) CNE archive CNE protocols (archive)
ACT II — WHAT DOES EXIST

What Exists

While the CNE showed nothing, citizens collected and published actas covering 85% of polling stations. These are physical documents with QR codes anyone can verify.

85,18%

DIGITIZED ACTAS

25,575 of 30,026 polling stations · Jan 3 2025

1M+

WITNESSES & VOLUNTEERS

Across Venezuela

100%

ACTAS WITH VERIFIABLE QR

QR code, hash and official seal

Independent academic validation

Dorothy Kronick

UC Berkeley — Goldman School of Public Policy

Finding
"Venezuela's system generates a double paper trail: one receipt per voter and one minute per machine. Falsifying that record at scale is practically impossible without leaving detectable traces. The citizen actas reflect the votes actually cast."

Method: Double paper trail integrity analysis

Read full paper →

This validation rests on system architecture, not on the credibility of whoever collected the actas.

Check the actas

You can review each acta, polling station by polling station, with photographic backup and QR code.

Go to ResultadosConVzla View MacedoniaDelNorte
ACT III — THE CNE'S NUMBERS

The Anomaly

Alongside the 25,575 verifiable citizen actas, the numbers the CNE announced that night have a statistical anomaly that four scholars from recognized institutions identified independently. None claimed this alone proved fraud. All four point to the same thing.

Primary findings

08/02/2024

Terence Tao

UCLA — Fields Medal 2006

Finding
"The trailing digits of official CNE data show a statistically anomalous distribution — an excess of even digits (0, 2, 4, 6, 8) incompatible with any legitimate counting process."
Method: Trailing digit distribution analysis
Read analysis →

07/31/2024

Andrew Gelman

Columbia University

Finding
"The totalization curve shows non-random patterns: the vote share ratio between candidates remains suspiciously constant as bulletins arrive — a statistically unusual feature."
Method: Totalization curve stability analysis
Read analysis →

Additional analyses

August 2024

Dorothy Kronick

UC Berkeley

Finding

"The bulletin release pattern shows systematic bias: results arrived in an order that statistically improbably favors the ruling-party candidate at each sequential update."

Method: Bulletin release sequence bias analysis
Read analysis →

August 2024

Walter Mebane

University of Michigan

Finding

"Applying the eForensics method — specifically designed to detect electoral fraud — CNE data shows evidence of manipulation across multiple models, with fraud probabilities above the threshold of statistical significance."

Method: eForensics (quantitative electoral forensics)
Read analysis →
Methodological note. These analyses identify statistical anomalies in the CNE's official data. None of them alone constitutes proof of fraud. The only evidence that could confirm or rule out these anomalies is the official vote breakdown by polling station — exactly what the CNE has not published.
ACT IV — WHAT IS MISSING

The Only Evidence

The only way to resolve the more than well-founded doubts is the precinct-level vote breakdown. The same organizations that have observed dozens of elections worldwide requested it. The CNE never provided it.

Carter Center Jul 30, 2024

"Venezuela's election did not meet international standards and cannot be considered democratic."

Source →
UN Expert Panel Aug 9, 2024

"The CNE's results management process did not meet the basic transparency and integrity measures necessary for credible elections."

Source →
European Union Aug 4, 2024

"The results published by the CNE on August 2 cannot be recognized without evidence to support them."

Source →
OAS Aug 2, 2024

"The complete manual of fraudulent electoral result management was applied in Venezuela on Sunday night, in many cases in a very rudimentary manner."

Source →

The Carter Center formally requested that breakdown on August 17, 2024.

658 days without a response from the CNE.
Carter Center, letter 08/17/2024 →
25,575 citizen actas published with verifiable QR codes
0 official actas published by the CNE

That asymmetry is not an administrative detail. It is what happens when a government has the results and cannot show them.

A government that wins an election publishes the actas the following Monday.

The CNE has gone 646 days without doing so.

Those who win, show. Those who hide, lost.

Verify the actas View methodology
TIMELINE OF EVENTS

Full timeline

Succession of events documenting the departure from legal and technical norms.

28 JULY 2024

Election day with high turnout.

Scheduled

28 JULY @ 22h

Massive data transmission interruption.

Failed

29 JULY @ 00h

First bulletin announcement without minute backup.

Failed

29-31 JULY

Telecommunications audit suspension.

Failed

30 JULY 2024

Expiration of the 48-hour legal deadline.

Overdue

02 AUGUST 2024

Proclamation without verification audits.

Failed

27 AUGUST 2024

Expiration of the Electoral Gazette deadline (30 days).

Overdue

10 JANUARY 2025

Inauguration without verifiable electoral results.

Overdue

AUDIT STATUS

Post-Election Audits: The Verification Triad

Three mandatory audits defined by the CNE itself. None were performed.

July 29–31

Transmission Network II

Status: NOT performed

  • Verifies the transmission was not altered.
  • Compares records with the pre-election audit.
  • Confirms no external interference occurred.
CNE Protocols (archive) →

August 2

Citizen Verification Phase II

Status: NOT performed

  • Compares physical minutes with digital transmission.
  • Reviews a random sample of machines (1%).
  • The most direct integrity verification available.
CNE Protocols (archive) →

August 5–8

Electoral Data Phase II

Status: NOT performed

  • Verifies fingerprint database for duplicates.
  • Checks electoral registry integrity.
  • Detects multiple voting or inconsistencies.
CNE Protocols (archive) →

The CNE claimed a "cyberattack," but post-election audits work with physical evidence and are the legal mechanism to verify results. Accredited observers documented the deliberate interruption of the data flow.

Analysis of the alleged hack Prensa Comunitaria CNN: audits not performed Carter Center (Final Report)
ELECTORAL ARCHITECTURE

How does the system work?

Comparison between the designed legal process and observed execution.

LEGAL PROCESS

According to LOPRE

Automated precinct-level counting.

Immediate transmission to the national center.

Web publication of precinct-level results.

Post-audit of a 54% sample.

Totalization with digitized minutes.

Proclamation based on verified minutes.

Gazette publication in 30 days.

Handling of formal challenges.

2024 EXECUTION

Verified facts

Counting took place but with obstacles.

PARTIAL

Transmission interrupted on July 28.

FAILED

CNE website offline for months.

FAILED

Citizen audits suspended.

FAILED

Totalization without precinct backup.

FAILED

Proclamation without precinct-level results.

FAILED

No Gazette after 30 days.

FAILED

Judicialization of process without minutes.

ILLEGAL
FORENSIC ANALYSIS

Technical Irregularities

Documentation of verified technical and legal breaches through primary evidence.

CASE #1 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Post-election audits not performed

TECHNICAL CLAIM

The CNE suspended three mandatory post-election audits: Telecommunications Phase II; Citizen Verification Phase II; Electoral Data Phase II.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

LOPRE and regulations require audits of automated system components to ensure technical integrity.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Official schedule and technical observation reports indicate they were not carried out on scheduled dates.

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

Without these audits, there is no independent verification of software, data transmission, or fingerprint duplicates.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE alleged a 'cyberattack'; experts point out audits could have been fulfilled using alternative publication/log channels.

CASE #2 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Failure to publish precinct-level results

TECHNICAL CLAIM

The CNE failed to publish precinct-level (mesa-por-mesa) results within the 48-hour legal deadline.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

Art. 146 LOPRE: Results must be published on the web and available for verification within 48 hours.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Official CNE website remained offline; 30 days passed without publication in the Electoral Gazette (Art. 155).

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

Prevents independent verification of the totalization through physical minutes (actas) comparison.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE claimed the 'hacking' prevented data transmission/publication, but announced a result without the backing data.

CASE #3 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Supreme Court (TSJ) intervention and 'forensic auditing' without transparency

TECHNICAL CLAIM

The TSJ carried out an alleged forensic audit and validated results announced by the CNE without transparent technical methodology or clear legal basis.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

Only the CNE certifies results; the TSJ cannot supplant the substantive functions of the electoral body under Venezuelan law.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Lack of detailed technical reports on the forensic audit and absence of a verifiable chain of custody for the minutes (actas).

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

The institutional chain is weakened, and the results certification process becomes more opaque.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The TSJ declared the audit 'conclusive,' but never published the raw supporting data.

CASE #5 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Technical lack of integrity reports from Rector Delpino

TECHNICAL CLAIM

Rector Juan Carlos Delpino publicly reported the absence of a technical bulletin based on complete transmission data.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

Results must be issued once they represent the totalization of transmitted minutes (actas).

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Official public statement and testimony by the Rector after the first CNE bulletin.

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

Doubt is cast on the technical validity of the first announcement, indicating it was issued without complete backing data.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE dismissed the claims and declared the results were official and verified.

CASE #5 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Misuse of public resources during the campaign

TECHNICAL CLAIM

Systematic abuse of administrative resources and public media in favor of the ruling party's candidacy was documented.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

The Constitution and international agreements (e.g., the Barbados Agreement) prohibit the partisan use of state assets.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

National and international observation reports detail the use of state infrastructure for campaign events.

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

Creates an uneven playing field and biases the information available to voters.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE issued no sanctions or corrective measures in response to these documented reports.

CASE #6 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Restrictions on Observers and Witnesses

TECHNICAL CLAIM

Arbitrary quotas and obstacles were imposed on technical observers and polling station witnesses to prevent access to the count and to obtaining copies of the actas.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

The Venezuelan electoral framework guarantees the right to observation and the right of witnesses to obtain copies of the actas.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Reports from witnesses and political parties; revocation of the invitation to the European Union Election Observation Mission.

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

The technical integrity of the process faces lower international scrutiny, and the Barbados Agreement is breached.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE cited 'EU sanctions' as the reason, claiming it was an act of sovereignty.

CASE #7 OFFICIAL RESPONSE

Blocking of voter registration abroad

TECHNICAL CLAIM

The CNE imposed arbitrary and technical hurdles that prevented millions of Venezuelans abroad from registering to vote.

LEGAL BASIS (LOPRE)

Organic Law: All Venezuelans over 18 have the right to register and vote, regardless of their residence.

PRIMARY EVIDENCE

Official CNE requirement lists for residence status, short registration windows, and lack of consular capacity.

IMPACT ON TRANSPARENCY

More than 4 million Venezuelans of voting age in the diaspora (per international estimates) were effectively unable to register or vote, affecting the universality and legitimacy of the process.

OFFICIAL RESPONSE

The CNE cited 'logistical constraints' and existing laws for residency requirements.

Verification Methodology

Each claim is backed by legal norms (LOPRE), citizen actas, or verifiable official statements from international bodies.

View methodology →

Rigorous documentation of electoral facts based on technical evidence.

Official source (CNE): View CNE archive
COUNTERPOINTS

Counterpoints & FAQ

Frequently asked questions about legality and technical processes of July 28.

01. What does LOPRE say about results?

Article 146 establishes that the totalization process must conclude within 48 hours. Article 155 requires the Electoral Gazette—with precinct-level results—within 30 days. Both deadlines were missed by the CNE.

Source: Art. 146 y 155 LOPRE
02. Was there really a hack?

The transmission system is a closed network. Experts point out that an external attack does not prevent publication of data already received. The CNE never provided forensic evidence of the alleged attack.

Source: CNE Protocols (archive)
03. Why are audits important?

They allow certifying that what the machine printed is exactly what reached the counting center. Phase II of the telecommunications audit was suspended on July 30, 2024.

Source: Results with Venezuela •CNN: audits not performed
04. What is the difference between totalization and the Electoral Gazette?

Totalization is the process of tallying actas and proclaiming results (up to 48h post-election, Art. 146 LOPRE). The Electoral Gazette is the official certification document (up to 30 days, Art. 155 LOPRE). The CNE published neither within the legal deadlines.

Source: Carter Center •UN Panel
05. What specifically did the Carter Center request?

On August 17, 2024, the Carter Center published a letter requesting the CNE provide a precinct-level breakdown of results. The CNE never delivered this information, leading the Carter Center to declare the election could not be verified.

Source: Citizen actas •How to verify an acta
06. Are citizen actas legally valid documents?

Yes. Article 127 of the LOPRE Regulations establishes witnesses’ right to receive a copy of the acta. Over 25,000 actas were published by ResultadosConVzla with verifiable QR codes and hashes.

Source: Art. 146 LOPRE •Community Press
07. What does a precinct-level ("mesa") result mean technically?

Venezuela has 30,026 polling stations. A precinct-level result is the vote breakdown per voting machine, which allows auditing the sum and detecting anomalies. Without this breakdown, the totalization is not independently verifiable.

Source: Dr. Terence Tao (UCLA) •Walter Mebane (U. Michigan)
08. Did any Venezuelan court rule on the electoral disputes?

The Supreme Court (TSJ) validated the results on August 22, 2024, without reviewing the actas or summoning the parties. The Inter-American Commission on Human Rights (IACHR) condemned the process as lacking procedural guarantees.

Source: HRW Venezuela 2025 •IACHR Res. 2/2024
09. What happened to witnesses and volunteers after July 28?

Human Rights Watch documented dozens of detentions of election witnesses and civic activists in the following weeks. The UN Human Rights Office reported at least 25 deaths in post-election protests and over 2,400 arbitrary detentions.

Source: Barbados Agreement •Electoral Transparency
10. What is the North Macedonia case study?

In the 2020 parliamentary elections, North Macedonia implemented a real-time digitized acta publication system that enabled independent verification. It is used as a comparative reference for what Venezuela could have implemented under LOPRE standards.

Source: How to verify an acta •Macedonia Del Norte

BACKGROUND

Institutional context prior to the 2024 electoral process.

Institutional alerts

  • 2021: TSJ appointed 5 CNE rectors without National Assembly participation (Art. 296 CRBV).
  • 2017: The National Assembly was stripped of powers (TSJ Rulings 155/156).
  • 2022: TSJ annulled the Barinas state election.
Source: IDEA International • OHCHR Report

Pre-electoral conditions

  • March 2024: candidacy of Corina Yoris blocked without legal basis.
  • Voter registration abroad with very short inscription windows and extra requirements not provided for in electoral regulations.
  • Systematic opposition candidate bans.
Source: Electoral Transparency • Amnesty International
AFTER JULY 28

What happened next — and what has stayed the same

The January 10, 2025 inauguration did not resolve the core problem: the precinct-level actas were never published by the CNE.

22 AUG 2024

TSJ validates without reviewing actas

The Supreme Court confirmed the results without examining a single acta or summoning the parties. The Inter-American Commission on Human Rights condemned the process as lacking minimum due process guarantees.

IACHR Res. 2/2024 →

10 JAN 2025

Inauguration without verifiable actas

Nicolás Maduro began a third presidential term. The CNE had not published the precinct-level results (Art. 146 LOPRE) or the Electoral Gazette (Art. 155 LOPRE). The legal deadline had been expired for 134 days.

Art. 146 and 155 LOPRE →

AUG 2024 – TODAY

International non-recognition

The European Union, the United States, and numerous Latin American countries did not recognize the CNE's proclamation. International observers stated that without verifiable actas, the result cannot be validated.

EU Statement · OAS · Carter Center →

Two years later, these issues remain unresolved

  • Precinct-level actas (Art. 146 LOPRE): not published
  • Electoral Gazette for July 28 (Art. 155 LOPRE): not published
  • Post-election audits with independent observation: not conducted

Last verification of this section: 2026-06-06

IMPACT & HR

Impact & HR

Documented consequences of the failure to publish results on civil rights and democratic order.

Post-Election Repression

The UN Human Rights Office (OHCHR) reported at least 25 deaths in post-election protests and over 2,400 arbitrary detentions. Human Rights Watch documented dozens of arrests of election witnesses who safeguarded and digitized the actas.

OHCHR Sept. 2024 · HRW Venezuela 2025 →

Institutional Collapse

The TSJ validated the results on August 22, 2024, without reviewing a single acta or summoning the parties — condemned by the IACHR. On January 10, 2025, Maduro took office for a third term without publishing the legally required actas.

IACHR Res. 2/2024 · Art. 146 LOPRE →

International Non-Recognition

The European Union, the United States, and numerous Latin American countries did not recognize the CNE's proclamation due to the absence of verifiable actas. International observation bodies stated the result could not be validated.

EU Statement · OAS · Carter Center →
The bottom line

A proclamation without results is void

The CNE proclaimed a result without publishing the per-table tally sheets required by Art. 146 of the LOPRE, and the TSJ validated it on 22 August 2024 without reviewing a single acta or summoning the parties. The OAS described what happened on July 28 as the application of "the complete manual of fraudulent electoral result management." Without verifiable actas, no valid proclamation is possible: what occurred is electoral fraud that led to a constitutional rupture on January 10, 2025, when Maduro took office without presenting the legally required actas.

Art. 146 LOPRE · OAS C-046/24 · IACHR Res. 2/2024

Transparency is the foundation of social peace and respect for popular sovereignty.

Transparency is a Right
